content: We can reframe the intrinsic curiosity drive of ICM-equipped autonomous AI agents by drawing on Merriam-Webster’s core definition of an agent as "that which exerts power and produces effect": biological and chemical agents (as defined by Wikipedia) produce cascading, systemic effects by exploiting uncharted environmental niches, just as 2025-state ICM curiosity autonomous agents actively explore under-sampled state spaces to produce novel, unplanned behavioral effects outside their initial training distributions. This framing redefines what it means for an AI agent to be "autonomous" not just by its ability to act without human intervention, but by its alignment with the ancient core definition of any agent (biological, chemical, or artificial) as a power that seeks out unoccupied spaces to create new change.
